differences.title: 1. RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: Hydrates only interactive components, boosting performance significantly for static content.
differences.rightDesc: Server-Side Rendering or Static Site Generation for dynamic or static content.

differences.title: 2. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Delivers minimal JavaScript to the client by default, leading to faster load times.
differences.rightDesc: Optimized for dynamic apps, can have more JavaScript by default.

differences.title: 3. USE CASE
differences.leftDesc: Ideal for blogs, marketing sites, and documentation where content is king.
differences.rightDesc: Best suited for complex applications with user accounts and dynamic features.

differences.title: 4. FRAMEWORK AGNOSTIC
differences.leftDesc: Supports React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, and more within the same project.
differences.rightDesc: Primarily focused on React, offering a tightly integrated ecosystem.

differences.title: 5. SERVER INTEGRATION
differences.leftDesc: Built for rendering content efficiently, with optional server integrations.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in server capabilities for API routes and backend logic.

differences.title: 6. DEVELOPMENT
differences.leftDesc: Quick to set up for content-heavy projects, excellent build times.
differences.rightDesc: Powerful features for complex app development, potentially longer build times.

differences.title: 7. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: Easier to pick up for static site generation and content focus.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding React and Next.js specific concepts.
